name: Chiang Mai
description: Thailand's cultural capital in the northern mountains — a haven for digital nomads, temple-hoppers, elephant lovers, and anyone craving a cooler, calmer Thai experience far from the beach crowds.

quickInfo.visa: Visa-on-arrival 30 days for most nationalities; e-Visa available

hotels.name: Bodega Chiang Mai Hostel
hotels.description: Party-friendly hostel on trendy Nimman Road with rooftop bar and great social scene

hotels.name: Tamarind Village
hotels.description: Boutique resort inside the Old City moat, wrapped around a 200-year-old tamarind tree

hotels.name: Akyra Manor Chiang Mai
hotels.description: Sleek boutique hotel steps from the city's coolest cafes, galleries and restaurants

hotels.name: Dhara Dhevi Chiang Mai
hotels.description: Thailand's most spectacular hotel — a recreated Lanna kingdom spreading across 60 acres

hotels.name: Four Seasons Resort Chiang Mai
hotels.description: Lush rice terraces, elephants, and mountain views — the most romantic luxury escape in Asia

activities.name: Ethical Elephant Sanctuary Half-Day
activities.description: Feed, bathe and walk with rescued elephants at a certified ethical no-riding sanctuary

activities.name: Doi Inthanon National Park Trekking
activities.description: Trek to Thailand's highest peak, twin royal pagodas, and misty hill-tribe villages

activities.name: Old City Temple Tour (Doi Suthep Included)
activities.description: Visit Doi Suthep, Wat Chedi Luang, and Wat Phra Singh with a knowledgeable guide

activities.name: Thai Cooking Class (Market + 4 Dishes)
activities.description: Shop at a local market then cook classic Northern Thai dishes in a garden kitchen

activities.name: Chiang Rai Golden Triangle Day Trip
activities.description: Visit the White Temple, Blue Temple, and the infamous Golden Triangle opium region

items.activity: Monks' alms giving ceremony at Wat Suan Dok
Old City temple circuit: Chedi Luang + Phra Singh
Khao Soi lunch (Chiang Mai's signature curry noodle)
Sunday Walking Street (or Night Bazaar weekdays)
Morning with rescued elephants — feeding & bathing
Return to city, rest and lunch
Thai massage (1.5 hours, ~$10 in Old City)
Dinner: Riverside restaurant with live music
Market visit for cooking class ingredients
Drive to Doi Inthanon National Park
Ang Ka Nature Trail rainforest walk
Back in Chiang Mai, farewell dinner

practicalInfo.gettingThere: Chiang Mai International Airport (CNX) has direct flights from Bangkok (1h15min), Singapore, Kuala Lumpur, and other regional hubs. Budget carriers AirAsia and Nok Air run frequent cheap flights.
practicalInfo.localTransport: Songthaew (red shared trucks) are the main transport — flat rate 30-50 THB. Grab app works well. Rent a scooter for $6/day or bicycle for $3/day in the city.
